tasks.title: Make web UI results loading scalable for large JSONL outputs
evidence: web/src/App.tsx:360-361: loadResults() uses response.text() and splits all lines, loading full results into memory.
web/src/App.tsx:364-371: parseItems loops through all lines and parses every JSON object into memory.
internal/api/server.go:524-530: /v1/jobs/{id}/results for jsonl format uses http.ServeFile() with no pagination.
internal/api/server.go:534-555: For json/md/csv formats, uses exporter.ExportStream() but still processes entire file.
api/openapi.yaml:459-508: /v1/jobs/{id}/results endpoint only has format parameter, no limit/offset pagination.
internal/exporter/exporter.go:404-417: exportJSONLStream streams input to output but cannot be used for pagination.
internal/exporter/exporter.go:534-553: parseLinesReader() loads all items into memory with no limit/offset support.
Large crawl results (1000+ pages) cause web UI to freeze or crash due to memory exhaustion.
plan: Add limit and offset query parameters to /v1/jobs/{id}/results endpoint in api/openapi.yaml for paginated JSONL responses.
Add Limit() and Offset() helper functions to internal/exporter/exporter.go to parse query parameters with defaults (limit=100, offset=0).
Add ExportPaginated() function to internal/exporter/exporter.go that returns a slice of items with offset/limit applied.
Update internal/api/server.go handleJobResults() to call ExportPaginated() for jsonl format when limit/offset params present.
Regenerate TS client with make generate to include new API parameters.
Update web/src/App.tsx loadResults() to track current page (page state variable) and request limit/offset from API.
Add pagination controls to web UI (Previous/Next buttons, page number display, jump to page input) in results view.
Update web/src/App.tsx to clear result items when format changes or new job is selected to avoid stale state.
Add tests in web/src/App.test.tsx for pagination (verify page changes, correct data per page, edge cases).
Add tests in internal/api/server_test.go for limit/offset query parameter handling.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Default page size of 100 items balances UI responsiveness with API load.
offset is 0-indexed: page=1 means offset=0, page=2 means offset=100, etc.
Client-side lazy loading is insufficient; must paginate at API level to avoid transferring huge payloads.
For formats other than jsonl (json/md/csv), pagination is less critical since these are typically single-item exports or small reports.
Consider adding X-Total-Count response header for total item count to enable 'Showing X-Y of Z results' display.

tasks.title: Reject unknown JSON fields in API requests for clearer UX
evidence: internal/api/server.go:210: handleScrape uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields, silently ignoring typos like 'ur' instead of 'url'.
internal/api/server.go:281: handleCrawl uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:354: handleResearch uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:585: handleAuthProfile (PUT) uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&profile) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server_test.go: No tests exist for unknown field validation; existing tests focus on missing fields, invalid values, and malformed JSON.
plan: Add decoder := json.NewDecoder(r.Body); decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() pattern to handleScrape at line 210.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleCrawl at line 281.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleResearch at line 354.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleAuthProfile (PUT) at line 585.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleAuthImport, handleAuthExport, handleSchedules at lines 629,657,699.
Add test in internal/api/server_test.go: TestUnknownFieldsRejected with subtests for /v1/scrape, /v1/crawl, /v1/research, /v1/schedules, and /v1/auth/profiles endpoints.
Verify that unknown fields return 400 with clear error message containing 'unknown field' or 'json: unknown field'.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Go json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() returns json.UnmarshalTypeError for unknown fields, which should trigger the existing error handling at line 211 (handleScrape), 282 (handleCrawl), 355 (handleResearch), etc.
The writeJSONError helper already returns 400 status for 'invalid json' errors, so DisallowUnknownFields errors will be handled correctly without code changes.
Only applies to POST/PUT endpoints with request bodies: handleScrape, handleCrawl, handleResearch, handleSchedules (POST), handleAuthProfile (PUT), handleAuthImport, handleAuthExport.
